creator: winter@jurai.net
text:
Pine4 should be installed so that it does not conflict with pine/pine3.

bin/pine -> pine4
bin/pico -> pico4
bin/pilot -> pilot4
lib/libpico.so.1.3 -> libpico.so.4.1 (would 4.0.1 be valid?)
man/man1/pine.1.gz -> pine4.1.gz
man/man1/pico.1.gz -> pico4.1.gz
man/man1/pilot.1.gz -> pilot4.1.gz

May I also suggest that the pine3 port be changed in the same way.

The shared library name change is important because the old pine will 
not work with the new libpico.

I would suggest a 'make install-default' target be created that would
symlink the 'base name' to the version specific name:

pine -> pine{3,4}

etc...

Fix: 

Most of it should be fairly simple, save the install-default stuff which
should really see further discussion as it impacts more than just pine.

If someone doesn't want to do a quick s/r and produce a patchfile for
pico/makefile.bsf I'll provide the necessary patches.

Just let me know.
How-To-Repeat: Install pine3 and pine4 on the same system.

Attempt to run pine3 after pine4 has been installed.
